key_insight: Raising argon flow from 1600 to 1800 L/h in air plasma spraying produces thicker, denser NiCrFeBSiC coatings with better tribological performance.
engineering_relevance: The result gives a practical process window for tuning plasma-sprayed self-fluxing coatings to improve wear resistance and coating quality.
method: Air plasma sprayed NiCrFeBSiC self-fluxing metal-ceramic coatings
Compared two argon flow rates: 1600 and 1800 L/h
Evaluated structure, microhardness, elastic modulus, friction, and adhesion
evidence_signal: At 1800 L/h, coating thickness increased by about 2×, porosity dropped by about 2×, and the friction coefficient decreased to approximately 0.107.
watch_item: The study compares only two argon flow settings, so broader process optimization and long-term wear behavior still need confirmation.
paper_title: EFFECT OF ARGON FLOW RATE DURING AIR PLASMA SPRAYING ON THE STRUCTURE AND PROPERTIES OF NICRFEBSIC COATINGS
